response.justification: Wei Chen is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine tooling interests — including direct IDE development experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) and authorship of developer tools like a test runner and assertion library. However, the role's core requirements are strong Java/Kotlin expertise, deep JVM understanding, and solid Spring ecosystem experience, and the resume shows only incidental Java use and no Kotlin, Spring, or enterprise JVM framework work. The career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, making the fit for an enterprise JVM tooling role weak despite otherwise strong craftsmanship.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI in C/C++)
Author of developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts) showing genuine language-tooling interest
Long track record across complex systems, low-latency services, and strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only minor, dated Java exposure — core requirement unmet
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework background
Primary stack (TypeScript/Node, DLang) is far from JVM internals and build-tool/app-server tooling
